words.definitionEn: used before countable or singular nouns referring to people or things that have not already been mentioned
words.exampleEn: a man/horse/unit

words.definitionEn: including all the parts, etc. that are necessary; whole
words.exampleEn: a complete list/sequence/picture/profile

words.definitionEn: to finish making or doing something
words.exampleEn: to complete a course/project

words.definitionEn: an electronic machine that can store, organize and find information, do processes with numbers and other data, and control other machines
words.exampleEn: a home computer

words.definitionEn: a public performance of music
words.exampleEn: to attend/go to a concert

words.definitionEn: a fully grown person who is legally responsible for their actions
words.exampleEn: Children must be accompanied by an adult.

words.definitionEn: an informal talk involving a small group of people or only two; the activity of talking in this way
words.exampleEn: a phone conversation

words.definitionEn: an opinion or a suggestion about what somebody should do in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: expert/practical/professional/medical advice

words.definitionEn: to prepare food by heating it, for example by boiling, baking or frying it
words.exampleEn: Where did you learn to cook?

words.definitionEn: the process of preparing food by heating it
words.exampleEn: My husband does all the cooking.

words.definitionEn: fairly cold; not hot or warm
words.exampleEn: cool air/water

words.definitionEn: accurate or true, without any mistakes
words.exampleEn: Do you have the correct time?

words.definitionEn: to make something right or accurate, for example by changing it or removing mistakes
words.exampleEn: correct something, to correct an error

words.definitionEn: the amount of money that you need in order to buy, make or do something
words.exampleEn: cost of something, the high/low cost of housing

words.definitionEn: if something costs a particular amount of money, you need to pay that amount in order to buy, make or do it
words.exampleEn: cost something, How much did it cost?

words.definitionEn: used as the past tense of ‘can’
words.exampleEn: She said that she couldn't come.

words.definitionEn: an area of land that has or used to have its own government and laws
words.exampleEn: European countries

words.definitionEn: a series of lessons or lectures on a particular subject
words.exampleEn: a French/chemistry course

words.definitionEn: a child of your aunt or uncle
words.exampleEn: She's my cousin.

words.definitionEn: a large animal kept on farms to produce milk or beef
words.exampleEn: to milk a cow

words.definitionEn: the thick white or pale yellow fatty liquid that rises to the top of milk, used in cooking or as a type of sauce to put on fruit, etc.
words.exampleEn: We had strawberries and cream for dessert.

words.definitionEn: to make something happen or exist
words.exampleEn: Scientists disagree about how the universe was created.

words.definitionEn: feeling fear; frightened because you think that you might be hurt or suffer
words.exampleEn: Don't be afraid.

words.definitionEn: the customs and beliefs, art, way of life and social organization of a particular country or group
words.exampleEn: African/American/European/Islamic culture

words.definitionEn: a small container that is like a bowl in shape, usually with a handle, used for drinking tea, coffee, etc.
words.exampleEn: He filled the cup with water.

words.definitionEn: later than something; following something in time
words.exampleEn: We'll leave after lunch.

words.definitionEn: a person or an organization that buys goods or services from a shop or business
words.exampleEn: marketing strategies to target potential customers

words.definitionEn: to make an opening or a wound in something, especially with a sharp tool such as a knife or scissors
words.exampleEn: cut something, She cut her finger on a piece of glass.

words.definitionEn: father
words.exampleEn: That's my dad over there.

words.definitionEn: the period of time from 12 o'clock in the middle of the day until about 6 o’clock in the evening
words.exampleEn: this/yesterday/tomorrow afternoon

words.definitionEn: a series of movements and steps that are usually performed to music; a particular example of these movements and steps
words.exampleEn: Do you know any other Latin American dances?

words.definitionEn: to move your body to the sound and rhythm of music
words.exampleEn: Do you want to dance?

words.definitionEn: a person who dances or whose job is dancing
words.exampleEn: She's a fantastic dancer.

words.definitionEn: moving your body to music
words.exampleEn: There was music and dancing till two in the morning.

words.definitionEn: likely to injure or harm somebody, or to damage or destroy something
words.exampleEn: The situation is extremely dangerous.

words.definitionEn: with no or very little light, especially because it is night
words.exampleEn: a dark room/street/forest/night

words.definitionEn: a particular day of the month, sometimes in a particular year, given in numbers and words
words.exampleEn: ‘What's the date today?’ ‘The 10th.’

words.definitionEn: one more time; on another occasion
words.exampleEn: This must never happen again.

words.definitionEn: a person’s female child
words.exampleEn: We have two sons and a daughter.

words.definitionEn: a period of 24 hours
words.exampleEn: ‘What day is it today?’ ‘Monday.’

words.definitionEn: used at the beginning of a letter before the name or title of the person that you are writing to
words.exampleEn: Dear Sir or Madam

words.definitionEn: the 12th and last month of the year
words.exampleEn: She was born in December.

words.definitionEn: the number of years that a person has lived or a thing has existed
words.exampleEn: You're the same age as my brother.

words.definitionEn: to think carefully about the different possibilities that are available and choose one of them
words.exampleEn: It's up to you to decide.

words.definitionEn: having a very pleasant taste or smell
words.exampleEn: Who cooked this? It's absolutely delicious.

words.definitionEn: used in expressions of time with the simple past tense to show how far in the past something happened
words.exampleEn: two weeks/months/years ago

words.definitionEn: to say what somebody/something is like
words.exampleEn: describe somebody/something, The next section describes our findings in detail.

words.definitionEn: a piece of writing or speech that says what somebody/something is like; the act of writing or saying in words what somebody/something is like
words.exampleEn: Follow the link below for a more detailed description.

words.definitionEn: the general arrangement of the different parts of something that is made, such as a building, book, machine, etc.
words.exampleEn: The basic design of the car is very similar to that of earlier models.

words.definitionEn: to have the same opinion as somebody; to say that you have the same opinion
words.exampleEn: When he said that, I had to agree.

words.definitionEn: to decide how something will look, work, etc., by drawing plans, making computer models, etc.
words.exampleEn: design something, He designed and built his own house.

words.definitionEn: a piece of furniture like a table, often with drawers (= parts like boxes in it with handles on the front for pulling them open), that you sit at to read, write, work, etc.
words.exampleEn: at a desk, I spend all day sitting at a desk.

words.definitionEn: a small individual fact or item; a less important fact or item
words.exampleEn: an expedition planned down to the last detail

words.definitionEn: a conversation in a book, play or film or in language teaching materials
words.exampleEn: Learners are asked to listen to three short dialogues.

words.definitionEn: a book or electronic resource that gives a list of the words of a language in alphabetical order and explains what they mean, or gives a word for them in a foreign language
words.exampleEn: a Spanish-English dictionary

words.definitionEn: to stop living
words.exampleEn: My father died suddenly at the age of 48.

words.definitionEn: the food and drink that you eat and drink regularly
words.exampleEn: to eat a healthy, balanced diet

words.definitionEn: the way in which two people or things are not like each other; the way in which somebody/something has changed
words.exampleEn: Americans and Brits speak the same language, but there are big cultural differences.

words.definitionEn: not the same as somebody/something; not like somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: different from somebody/something, American English is significantly different from British English.

words.definitionEn: not easy; needing effort or skill to do or to understand
words.exampleEn: The competition judges were given a very difficult task.

words.definitionEn: the main meal of the day, eaten either in the middle of the day or in the evening
words.exampleEn: It's time for dinner.

words.definitionEn: not clean
words.exampleEn: dirty hands/clothes/dishes

words.definitionEn: to talk about something with somebody, especially in order to decide something
words.exampleEn: discuss something with somebody, Have you discussed the problem with anyone?

words.definitionEn: a flat shallow container for cooking food in or serving it from
words.exampleEn: a baking/serving dish

words.definitionEn: the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth and that we breathe
words.exampleEn: Let's go out for some fresh air.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to actions that you do not mention by name or do not know about
words.exampleEn: What are you doing this evening?

words.definitionEn: used before a full verb to form negative sentences and questions
words.exampleEn: I don't like fish.

words.definitionEn: a person who has been trained in medical science, whose job is to treat people who are ill or injured
words.exampleEn: You'd better see a doctor about that cough.

words.definitionEn: a place where planes land and take off and that has buildings for passengers to wait in
words.exampleEn: Gatwick Airport

words.definitionEn: an animal with four legs and a tail, often kept as a pet or trained for work, for example hunting or guarding buildings. There are many types of dog, some of which are wild.
words.exampleEn: I took the dog for a walk.

words.definitionEn: the unit of money in the US, Canada, Australia and several other countries
words.exampleEn: You will be paid in American dollars.

words.definitionEn: a piece of wood, glass, etc. that is opened and closed so that people can get in and out of a room, building, car, etc.; a similar thing in a cupboard
words.exampleEn: Open the door!

words.definitionEn: to or at a lower place or position
words.exampleEn: She jumped down off the chair.

words.definitionEn: from a high or higher point on something to a lower one
words.exampleEn: The stone rolled down the hill.

words.definitionEn: down the stairs; on or to a floor of a house or building lower than the one you are on, especially the one at ground level
words.exampleEn: I couldn't sleep so I went downstairs and watched TV.

words.definitionEn: to make pictures, or a picture of something, with a pencil, pen or chalk (but not paint)
words.exampleEn: You draw beautifully.

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing that is made in one piece and hangs down to cover the body as far as the legs, sometimes reaching to below the knees, or to the ankles
words.exampleEn: a long white dress

words.definitionEn: to put clothes on yourself/somebody
words.exampleEn: I dressed quickly.

words.definitionEn: a liquid for drinking; an amount of a liquid that you drink
words.exampleEn: Can I have a drink?

words.definitionEn: to take liquid into your mouth and then down your throat into your stomach
words.exampleEn: drink something, I don't drink coffee.

words.definitionEn: to operate a vehicle so that it goes in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: Can you drive?

words.definitionEn: a person who drives a vehicle
words.exampleEn: a bus/train/taxi driver

words.definitionEn: all through a period of time
words.exampleEn: during the 1990s

words.definitionEn: a disk on which large amounts of information, especially films, photographs and video, can be stored, for use on a DVD player or computer (the abbreviation for ‘digital versatile disc’ or ‘digital videodisc’)
words.exampleEn: Let's just stay in and watch a DVD.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to every one of two or more people or things, when you are thinking about them separately
words.exampleEn: Each answer is worth 20 points.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to every one of two or more people or things, when you are thinking about them separately
words.exampleEn: Each answer is worth 20 points.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to every one of two or more people or things, when you are thinking about them separately
words.exampleEn: Each answer is worth 20 points.

words.definitionEn: either of the organs on the sides of the head that you hear with
words.exampleEn: the left/right ear

words.definitionEn: near the beginning of a period of time, an event etc.
words.exampleEn: the early morning

words.definitionEn: near the beginning of a period of time, an event, a piece of work, etc.
words.exampleEn: We arrived early the next day.

words.definitionEn: in or towards the east
words.exampleEn: East Africa

words.definitionEn: towards the east
words.exampleEn: The house faces east.

words.definitionEn: the direction that you look towards to see the sun rise; one of the four main points of the compass
words.exampleEn: Which way is east?

words.definitionEn: not difficult; done or obtained without a lot of effort or problems
words.exampleEn: an easy way to make bread

words.definitionEn: to put food in your mouth, bite it and swallow it
words.exampleEn: I was too nervous to eat.

words.definitionEn: a small oval object with a thin hard shell produced by a female bird and containing a young bird; a similar object (without a hard shell) produced by a female fish, insect, etc.
words.exampleEn: The female sits on the eggs until they hatch.

words.definitionEn: a very large animal with thick grey skin, large ears, two curved outer teeth called tusks and a long nose called a trunk. There are two types of elephant, the African and the Asian.
words.exampleEn: herds of elephants
There are only eleven of these rare animals left.

words.definitionEn: in addition to something already mentioned
words.exampleEn: What else did he say?

words.definitionEn: a way of sending messages and data to other people by means of computers connected together in a network
words.exampleEn: I will be on vacation for a week in November, with no access to email.

words.definitionEn: to send a message to somebody by email
words.exampleEn: email somebody, Patrick emailed me yesterday.

words.definitionEn: the whole number of
words.exampleEn: All horses are animals, but not all animals are horses.

words.definitionEn: the whole number or amount
words.exampleEn: All of the food has gone.

words.definitionEn: the final part of a period of time, an event, an activity or a story
words.exampleEn: It's the end of an era.

words.definitionEn: to finish; to reach a point and go no further; to make something finish
words.exampleEn: At last the war ended.

words.definitionEn: to get pleasure from something
words.exampleEn: enjoy something, We thoroughly enjoyed our time in New York.

words.definitionEn: to the necessary degree
words.exampleEn: I hadn't trained enough for the game.

words.definitionEn: used before plural or uncountable nouns to mean ‘as many or as much as somebody needs or wants’
words.exampleEn: Have you made enough copies?

words.definitionEn: as many or as much as somebody needs or wants
words.exampleEn: Six bottles should be enough.

words.definitionEn: the unit of money of some countries of the European Union
words.exampleEn: I paid five euros for it.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize something unexpected or surprising
words.exampleEn: She didn't even call to say she wasn't coming.

words.definitionEn: the part of the day between the afternoon and the time you go to bed
words.exampleEn: Remember to call me this evening.

words.definitionEn: a thing that happens, especially something important
words.exampleEn: recent/current/world events

words.definitionEn: used in negative sentences and questions, or sentences with if to mean ‘at any time’
words.exampleEn: Nothing ever happens here.

words.definitionEn: used with singular nouns to refer to all the members of a group of things or people
words.exampleEn: She knows every student in the school.

words.definitionEn: every person; all people
words.exampleEn: Everybody knows Tom.

words.definitionEn: every person; all people
words.exampleEn: Everyone cheered and clapped.

words.definitionEn: all things
words.exampleEn: Everything had gone.

words.definitionEn: a formal written, spoken or practical test, especially at school or college, to see how much you know about a subject, or what you can do
words.exampleEn: to take an exam

words.definitionEn: something such as an object, a fact or a situation that shows, explains or supports what you say
words.exampleEn: Let me give you an example.

words.definitionEn: feeling or showing happiness and enthusiasm
words.exampleEn: Staff got excited when they heard they were getting a bonus.

words.definitionEn: causing great interest or excitement
words.exampleEn: This is an exciting opportunity for me.

words.definitionEn: physical or mental activity that you do to stay healthy or become stronger
words.exampleEn: Swimming is good exercise.

words.definitionEn: to do sports or other physical activities in order to stay healthy or become stronger; to make an animal do this
words.exampleEn: an hour’s class of exercising to music

words.definitionEn: costing a lot of money
words.exampleEn: an expensive car/restaurant/holiday

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody about something in a way that makes it easy to understand
words.exampleEn: explain (something), He was trying to explain the difference between hip hop and rap.

words.definitionEn: more than is usual, expected, or than exists already
words.exampleEn: extra money/cash/funding

words.definitionEn: in addition; too
words.exampleEn: She's fluent in French and German. She also speaks a little Italian.

words.definitionEn: either of the two organs on the face that you see with
words.exampleEn: The suspect has dark hair and green eyes.

words.definitionEn: the front part of the head, where the eyes, nose and mouth are
words.exampleEn: a pretty/pale/round face

words.definitionEn: a thing that is known to be true, especially when it can be proved
words.exampleEn: fact about something, First, some basic facts about healthy eating.

words.definitionEn: to drop down from a higher level to a lower level
words.exampleEn: The rain was falling steadily.
